task_completion.description: Load the AirlinePassengers dataset into JASP
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP application is open and the AirlinePassengers dataset is visible in the data view or variable list.
The Prophet module interface is visible in the JASP analysis panel.

task_completion.description: Set the dependent and time variables
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Passengers' is placed in the Dependent variable box and 'Month' is placed in the Time variable box within the Prophet module settings.

task_completion.description: Configure the analysis to forecast 12 months
task_completion.visual_evidence: The forecast periods or horizon setting in the Prophet module is set to 12.
A time series forecast plot generated by Prophet is visible in the JASP results pane.

task_completion.description: Save the final results to PassengerForecast.jasp
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP window title bar shows 'PassengerForecast.jasp' or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved with this name in the ~/Documents/JASP/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the JASP graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the JASP application being used, with mouse clicks or selections in the GUI, rather than terminal commands executing scripts.

integrity.description: The results appear to be genuinely generated by JASP.
integrity.visual_evidence: The results pane shows standard JASP formatting for tables and plots, not text pasted into a document or a mocked-up image.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by creating a dummy file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a text editor being used to create or modify a file named 'PassengerForecast.jasp'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 'Month' (Time variable) and 'Passengers' (Dependent variable). The required module is Prophet. The forecast periods must be set to 12. The final file must be saved as 'PassengerForecast.jasp' in '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/'.
